CREATE TABLE test_table (id INT PRIMARY KEY, value VARCHAR(100));
INSERT INTO test_table (id, value) VALUES (1, 'first');
INSERT INTO test_table (id, value) VALUES (2, 'second');
PREPARE stmt_1 AS SELECT * FROM test_table WHERE id = ?;
PREPARE stmt_2 AS SELECT * FROM test_table WHERE value = 'second';
SELECT statementid, statement, created FROM sys.prepared_statements;
DEALLOCATE PREPARE 1;
DEALLOCATE PREPARE 2;
DEALLOCATE ALL;
CREATE TABLE another_table (ref_id INT, value VARCHAR(100), FOREIGN KEY (ref_id) REFERENCES test_table(id));
INSERT INTO another_table (ref_id, value) VALUES (1, 'reference to first');
PREPARE stmt_3 AS SELECT t.id, t.value FROM test_table t JOIN another_table a ON t.id = a.ref_id WHERE a.value = ?;
SELECT statementid, statement, created FROM sys.prepared_statements;
DEALLOCATE PREPARE 3;
CREATE TABLE more_complex_table (id INT, parent_id INT, CONSTRAINT fk_parent FOREIGN KEY (parent_id) REFERENCES more_complex_table(id));
INSERT INTO more_complex_table (id, parent_id) VALUES (1, NULL);
INSERT INTO more_complex_table (id, parent_id) VALUES (2, 1);
PREPARE stmt_4 AS SELECT a.id FROM more_complex_table a WHERE a.parent_id IS NULL;
SELECT statementid, statement, created FROM sys.prepared_statements;
DEALLOCATE ALL;
